description_long: This is a two-story brick apartment building with a hipped, dark brown roof and a stone foundation. The facade features symmetrical window arrangements, with three windows on the upper floor and two on the lower, flanking a central entrance door. The windows are rectangular and framed in white, providing a clean contrast to the reddish-brown brick exterior. The roof includes a small chimney and a vent, and the building sits on a stone base that wraps around all sides. There are no balconies or decorative elements, giving the structure a functional and straightforward appearance. The overall design is practical, with a focus on durability and simplicity, typical of suburban or urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A two-story brick apartment building with a hipped dark roof, symmetrical windows, and a stone foundation. The design is simple and functional, with a central entrance and no balconies.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, three upper windows, two lower windows, and the stone foundation.
description_view_1: Side view with four windows (two per floor), the chimney visible on the roof, and no entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view with three windows (two upper, one lower), a vent on the roof, and no entrance or decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with four windows (two per floor), the chimney, and no entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows, with consistent spacing and alignment across both floors on all sides of the building.
